title: iris reticulata var. bakeriana
intro: Iris reticulata var. bakeriana, also known as the Baker's iris, is a type of flowering bulbous perennial plant native to Iran. It is a rhizomatous iris variety with striking, vibrant flowers in shades of purple and white. The Baker's iris is used in herbal medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and digestive issues. The plant can be prepared as a tincture, decoction, or salve, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and soothing skin irritations.
